title: portulaca pilosa subsp. decipiens
intro: Portulaca pilosa subsp. decipiens, also known as annual purslane or pigweed, is a subspecies of the annual purslane plant, native to tropical Asia. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, which help to soothe wounds and treat various health conditions. The plant's health benefits include treating fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as its use as an antiseptic and astringent. In herbal preparations, Portulaca pilosa subsp. decipiens is often dried and made into tea, or used in combination with other herbs to create topical creams and ointments for wound healing and skin conditions.
